title: With Pasta, Infrastructure container entry doesn't manifest
body:
### Issue Description

When using `--net=pasta` with a pod the invocation fails to set the infrastructure container hostname. Instead the pod name is set in front of the host name of the next container added to the pod.

I noticed this when attempting to deploy [immich](https://github.com/immich-app/immich), which requires several containers and local hostname resolution within the pod.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

(WORKING) Without Pasta:

```
podman pod create --name immich --publish 127.0.0.1:3300:8080

podman run --replace --detach --pod=immich --name=redis --label "io.containers.autoupdate=image" docker.io/library/redis:latest

podman exec -it redis bash

# cat /etc/hosts 
127.0.0.1	localhost localhost.localdomain localhost4 localhost4.localdomain4
::1	localhost localhost.localdomain localhost6 localhost6.localdomain6
172.22.4.4	host.containers.internal
10.0.2.100	immich 3c0d761ba169-infra
127.0.0.1	redis
```
(FAILURE) With Pasta:

```
podman pod create --name immich --net=pasta --publish 127.0.0.1:3300:8080

podman run --replace --detach --pod=immich --name=redis --label "io.containers.autoupdate=image" docker.io/library/redis:latest

podman exec -it redis bash

# cat /etc/hosts 
127.0.0.1	localhost localhost.localdomain localhost4 localhost4.localdomain4
::1	localhost localhost.localdomain localhost6 localhost6.localdomain6
172.22.4.4	host.containers.internal
127.0.0.1	immich redis
```



### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

I'd expected as I added more containers to the pod to have name resolution work, however whatever container is first added to the pod when using `--net=pasta` has its hostname effectively mangled.

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Andrew, thanks for reporting this.

I'm not familiar with the name resolution requirements of `immich`. What is exactly the resolution request that needs to be answered differently, or the missing/wrong entry in `/etc/hosts` here?

That is, would you expect `3c0d761ba169-infra` to be resolved, in the container, as `127.0.0.1` and `::1`? Or as the non-loopback addresses given to the container?

See https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/78f1ebb80ba473f4f9bb016aec5d190645089cce/libpod/container_internal_common.go#L2143-L2162 for the current selection, I guess we simply missed adding pasta there.

The only problem is that pasta does not use a static ip, we would actually have to join the netns to get the correct ip for the entry. Not a big deal just some extra steps.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Assuming I'll have to fix this: no progress yet, but I'm not blocked either, @Luap99 already outlined the steps.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@Luap99, Stefano has been super busy, so I've been taking a look at this.  I'm not familiar enough yet with podman internals to know what I'd need to do to enter the namespace and probe the IP in the pasta case.  Can you give me any pointers here?

I was about to work on this, if you want to take it that is good for me as well.

I already fixed that in buildah you basically just need to do this: https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/6589be3ba189107e9389c8454951ff38fe9692ab/run_linux.go#L536-L544
The netns path is stored in `c.state.NetNS`

and just add the IsPasta() case to the switch in `getHostsEntries()` I already linked above.

@Luap99 thanks.  I've sent a PR, which I think does the right thing.

I am a bit concerned this may be fragile - I see an entry for the host's public IP in /etc/hosts as well.  This is likely to be the same as the pod IP with pasta, which may break things that need to communicate with both.

I assume you are talking about host.containers.internal? This will definitely not work correctly right now and is worth a separate discussion. This is much more involved as it depends on the arguments used to start pasta.
